Apple Inc. Q1 Fiscal 2025 Earnings Call Transcript

Operator: Good afternoon. Welcome to Apple's Q1 Fiscal Year 2025 Earnings Conference Call.

Tim Cook, CEO:

Thank you. We are pleased to report record first-quarter revenue of $124.3 billion, up 4% year-over-year. iPhone revenue was $69.1 billion, essentially flat as the smartphone market remained saturated in key markets.

Services revenue reached a new all-time high of $23.1 billion, growing 14% year-over-year, driven by advertising, App Store, and Apple TV+. This is our fastest-growing segment and carries significantly higher margins than hardware.

We are seeing strong traction with Apple Intelligence, our on-device AI platform. However, the rollout has been slower than expected in key international markets due to regulatory requirements in the EU and China.

Greater China revenue declined 11% year-over-year to $20.8 billion, marking the third consecutive quarter of declines in the region. Competition from Huawei's Mate 70 series with Kirin 9100 chip has intensified significantly.

Luca Maestri, CFO:

Gross margin was 46.9%, up from 45.9% a year ago, driven by favorable Services mix. We returned $25 billion to shareholders through dividends and buybacks.

Operating expenses were $15.4 billion, up 8% year-over-year, largely driven by AI and machine learning R&D investments. We expect OpEx to continue rising as we invest in Apple Intelligence.

Inventory days were 8, consistent with our lean inventory management approach. We see no signs of channel stuffing or excess inventory.

Q&A highlights:
- Analyst from JPMorgan asked about Vision Pro trajectory — Cook noted "early innings" but did not provide unit sales figures
- Goldman Sachs asked about AI monetization timeline — management indicated meaningful revenue contribution expected by fiscal 2026
- Morgan Stanley asked about China strategy — Cook emphasized long-term commitment but acknowledged competitive headwinds
